explanation: Polyglyceryl-10 stearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and stearic acid, used to create stable, mild oil-in-water emulsions in natural and clean-formula skincare. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and a preferred PEG-free emulsifier in sensitive-skin formulations.

Polyglyceryl-2 dipolyhydroxystearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester used as a water-in-oil emulsifier, particularly effective in mineral sunscreen formulas for dispersing zinc oxide and titanium dioxide into an elegant, non-chalky texture. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, favored in natural and clean-formula sunscreen systems.

Polyglyceryl-2 stearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and stearic acid, used to create stable water-in-oil emulsions in natural-positioned skincare. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and favored in clean-formula systems for PEG-free emulsification.

Polyglyceryl-2 triisostearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and isostearic acid, used in water-in-oil and anhydrous formulas for stable PEG-free emulsification. It is well-tolerated and used in natural-positioned formulas as a mild emulsifier.

Polyglyceryl-3 diisostearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas and color cosmetics, providing stable emulsification with excellent skin compatibility. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, favored in natural and anhydrous formulas as a PEG-free emulsifier.

POLYGLYCERYL-3 DIISOSTEARATE 7743 is a grade-specified version of polyglyceryl-3 diisostearate — a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas and color cosmetics for stable emulsification. The trailing number indicates a trade grade specification; the ingredient is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Polyglyceryl-3 methylglucose distearate is a PEG-free sugar-derived emulsifier that provides stable, mild oil-in-water emulsification with additional skin-conditioning properties from its sugar component. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and widely used in natural-positioned and sensitive-skin formulas.

Polyglyceryl-3 polydimethylsiloxyethyl dimethicone is a PEG-free polyglyceryl-modified silicone copolymer emulsifier used in water-in-silicone formulas, providing silicone-phase emulsification without polyethylene glycol. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, valued in clean and natural-positioned formulas.

Polyglyceryl-3 polyricinoleate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and castor oil ricinoleic acid, widely used as a water-in-oil emulsifier in cosmetics and sunscreens. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, providing stable emulsification in anhydrous and oil-rich formulas.

Polyglyceryl-4 caprate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and capric acid (from coconut oil), used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer in cleansers and water-based formulas. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and used in natural-positioned and gentle cleansing formulas.

Polyglyceryl-4 isostearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas and color cosmetics for stable, skin-compatible emulsification. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, favored in natural and clean-formula systems.

Polyglyceryl-4 isostearate — the soft hyphens are a data artifact from ingredient label parsing — is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier used in water-in-oil formulas and color cosmetics for stable, skin-compatible emulsification. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Polyglyceryl-5 stearate is a PEG-free polyglyceryl ester emulsifier derived from glycerin and stearic acid, used to create stable oil-in-water emulsions in natural and clean-formula skincare. It is well-tolerated, biodegradable, and used in sensitive-skin formulations as a mild PEG-free emulsifier.

Polygonum cuspidatum (Japanese knotweed) root extract is the richest natural source of trans-resveratrol — a powerful stilbene antioxidant with documented anti-inflammatory, UV-protective, and anti-aging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and anti-aging formulas for its exceptional antioxidant potency and resveratrol content.

Polygonum multiflorum (he shou wu/fo-ti) extract is a traditional Chinese medicinal botanical providing stilbene glycosides and polyphenol antioxidants with anti-aging and skin-conditioning properties. It is generally well-tolerated at cosmetic extract concentrations and used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its antioxidant content.

Polyhydroxystearic acid is a branched, hydroxylated stearic acid polymer used as a lipophilic emulsifier and dispersant in water-in-oil formulas and mineral sunscreens, where it helps disperse zinc oxide and titanium dioxide particles for a more elegant, non-chalky texture. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Polymethyl methacrylate (PMMA) is a synthetic acrylate polymer used in cosmetics in fine sphere or powder form as a soft-focus texture agent and optical blurring ingredient in primers and foundations. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated — functioning as a sensory and optical modifier rather than a skin-active ingredient.

Polymethylsilsesquioxane is a crosslinked silicone resin powder used in cosmetics as a soft-focus texture agent, providing a velvety, pore-minimizing surface feel. It is considered well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and non-comedogenic — used in primers and foundations for its optical blurring effect.

Polyoxyl 40 stearate is equivalent to PEG-40 stearate — a PEG-modified stearate emulsifier used in skincare and pharmaceutical topical formulas to create stable oil-in-water emulsions. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the polyethylene glycol modification.

Polyphenylsilsesquioxane is a phenyl-modified silsesquioxane resin used in cosmetics as a gloss enhancer and texture modifier with a higher refractive index than standard silicone powders, providing a luminous, light-reflecting skin finish. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

Polyquaternium-10 is a cationic cellulose derivative used as a conditioning agent in hair care and skin care formulas, depositing a substantive film on negatively charged skin and hair surfaces to improve slip, softness, and moisture retention. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Polyquaternium-11 is a cationic vinyl pyrrolidone copolymer used as a conditioning film-former in hair and skin care formulas, providing a flexible, substantive film that improves feel and moisture retention. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical use concentrations.

Polyquaternium-51 (lipidure) is a phospholipid-structured cationic polymer that forms a moisture-retaining film on the skin surface structurally analogous to cell membrane phospholipids, providing exceptional biocompatible hydration. It is exceptionally well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in sensitive skin and contact lens comfort formulas for its biomimetic moisture-holding properties.

Polyquaternium-67 is a cationic quaternary polymer used as a conditioning and film-forming agent in hair care and skin care formulas, providing improved feel, slip, and moisture retention. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Polyquaternium-7 is a cationic acrylamide/DADMAC copolymer used as a conditioning agent in shampoos and skin cleansers, providing substantive deposition on skin and hair to improve feel and reduce friction after rinsing. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Polysorbate 20 is a PEG-modified sorbitan laurate widely used as a gentle emulsifier and solubilizer for fragrances, essential oils, and oil-soluble actives in water-based formulas. It is generally well-tolerated and considered one of the milder PEG-based emulsifiers; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the ethoxylated modification.

Polysorbate 60 is a PEG-modified sorbitan stearate used as a mild emulsifier in oil-in-water emulsions, providing stable emulsification with a smooth, skin-compatible texture. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the ethoxylated modification.

Polysorbate 80 is a PEG-modified sorbitan oleate widely used as a mild emulsifier and solubilizer for fragrances and oil-soluble ingredients in water-based formulas. It is generally well-tolerated; individuals with PEG sensitivity should note the ethoxylated modification.

Portulaca oleracea (purslane) extract is one of the richest plant sources of omega-3 fatty acids (ALA), along with flavonoids and betalain antioxidants with potent anti-inflammatory and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated — particularly on reactive, UV-stressed, and sensitive skin — and is used in soothing and protective formulas for its combined antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content.

Potassium alum (aluminum potassium sulfate) is a naturally occurring mineral astringent used as a deodorant and antimicrobial agent that inhibits odor-causing bacteria on the skin surface. It is considered well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations and marketed as a 'natural crystal deodorant' alternative to synthetic aluminum antiperspirants — though it still contains aluminum ions and is not free of the systemic aluminum absorption concerns associated with conventional antiperspirants.
